invitation.subject: {inviterName} invited you to {organizationName} on blueprnt
invitation.heading: Join {organizationName} on blueprnt
invitation.body: {inviterName} has invited you to {organizationName} on blueprnt. Accept the invitation below to set up your account and get started.
invitation.note: If you weren't expecting this invitation, you can ignore this email.

resetPassword.subject: Reset your password
resetPassword.heading: Reset your password
resetPassword.body: We received a request to reset the password for your blueprnt account. Click the button below to choose a new password.
resetPassword.note: If you didn't request this, you can safely ignore this email; your password won't change.

welcome.subject: Welcome to blueprnt
welcome.heading: Welcome to blueprnt
welcome.body: An account was created for you on blueprnt. Set your password below to get started.
welcome.note: If you weren't expecting this, you can ignore this email.

twoFactorCode.subject: Your blueprnt verification code
twoFactorCode.heading: Verify it's you
twoFactorCode.body: Enter this code to finish signing in to blueprnt:
twoFactorCode.note: This code expires shortly. If you didn't try to sign in, you can ignore this email and your account stays secure.

changeEmailConfirm.subject: Confirm your email change
changeEmailConfirm.heading: Confirm your email change
changeEmailConfirm.body: We received a request to change your blueprnt account email to {newEmail}. Click the button below to confirm the change from your current address.
changeEmailConfirm.note: If you didn't request this change, you can safely ignore this email. Your email address will not be changed.

verifyEmail.subject: Verify your new email address
verifyEmail.heading: Verify your new email address
verifyEmail.body: You're almost done. Click the button below to verify this address and complete your email change on blueprnt.
verifyEmail.note: If you weren't expecting this, you can ignore this email. No changes will be made to your account.

families.heading: Role families and roles at {name}
families.pasteHelpBody: Paste straight from a document, spreadsheet or HR system; the AI works out what you mean. If your text already mentions role families they are kept, otherwise the AI suggests which roles belong together in a family. You review the result and can change everything before anything is created.
families.placeholderPhrase1: Engineering: Software Developer, Tech Lead, Engineering Manager
families.placeholderPhrase2: Accountant, Payroll Specialist, HR Generalist, Office Manager
families.placeholderPhrase3: Store Manager, Sales Associate, Visual Merchandiser
families.dndInstructions: Press space or enter to pick up a role, use the arrow keys to move it, and press space or enter again to drop it. Press escape to cancel.
families.reviewHint: Group the roles into families and set each role's track.
families.reviewHintStarter: A starting point from your industry: group roles into families and set each role's track. Change anything later.
families.prefillingBody: We're writing a purpose and responsibilities for each role from its name. This can take a moment for a large set.

editor.heading: Criteria
editor.empty: No criteria yet. Add your first criterion or generate AI drafts.
editor.name: Name
editor.anchorsCaption: How a role is rated on this criterion. You set it when you evaluate a role.
editor.levelsIntro: Describe what a role looks like at each level, from 0 (lowest) to 5 (highest).
editor.pointsLeft: {count, plural, one {# weight point} other {# weight points}} left to distribute
editor.pointsOver: {count, plural, one {# weight point} other {# weight points}} over the budget
editor.minCriteriaHint: Add at least {min} criteria to continue.
editor.removalFloorHint: A model needs at least {min} criteria, so criteria cannot be removed right now.
editor.addDialogDescription: Describe what the criterion measures and write its evaluation scale. New criteria start at weight 3.
editor.editDialogDescription: Adjust the texts to your organization. The weight and existing ratings are not affected.
editor.removeDialogDescription: The criterion's ratings are removed from every role and its weight points are redistributed across the remaining criteria. This cannot be undone.

builder.defineDescription: Add the criteria roles are evaluated against, and describe what each rating 0 to 5 means.
builder.weightDescription: Decide how much each criterion counts. Weighting is not a verdict on whether a criterion is good: it is how much influence it has on role valuation and, all else equal, relative pay.
builder.weightMeaning1: Very low influence in the model
builder.weightMeaning2: Low to moderate influence in the model
builder.weightMeaning3: Clear and balanced influence in the model
builder.weightMeaning5: Very strong influence in the model

method.title: Method
method.description: Document why each criterion exists and its bias review, then export the method appendix.
method.dialogDescription: Record why this criterion belongs in the model and its bias review. This is evidence, not a guarantee.
method.approveHint: Fill in purpose, why relevant, bias risk, and a bias comment to approve.
method.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
method.draftError: The draft could not be generated. Try again.
method.aiAckLabel: This content was AI-generated. I confirm I have read and reviewed it.
method.approveAckLabel: I have reviewed this documentation and confirm it is accurate.

methodAppendix.draft: DRAFT: not all criteria are reviewed and approved
methodAppendix.methodologyBody: Roles are evaluated criterion by criterion on a 0 to 5 scale. Criterion weights sum to a fixed point budget; the role score is normalized to 0 to 100 and the band is derived, never set by hand. Ratings are hidden until every criterion is scored. Every change is recorded in the audit log. Roles describe work, never a person.
methodAppendix.biasStatement: This model is designed to reduce bias. It is bias-reducing, never bias-free.

ai.heading: AI assistance
ai.provenance: AI-generated suggestion. Review and confirm; nothing is applied automatically.
ai.noMoves: The AI found no moves to suggest.
ai.moveLabel: Move {points, plural, one {# weight point} other {# weight points}} from {from} to {to}

help.criterionBody: A criterion is something every role is evaluated against, like scope or complexity. Its 0 to 5 evaluation scale describes what each rating means; separately, its weighting decides how much it counts in the overall result.
help.anchorsLabel: How does the evaluation scale work?
help.anchorsBody: This is the 0 to 5 scale a role is evaluated against on this criterion. Each level describes what a role looks like there: write them concrete and role-focused, clearly increasing from 0 (not present) to 5 (the strongest reasonable expression), so two assessors reading the same job profile land on the same rating. This scale is about how a role is rated, not how much the criterion counts: that is its separate weighting.
help.anchorRoleBody: An anchor role is a reference role your organization has assessed and agreed on. Use the few you designate (2 to 5 is usually enough) to sanity-check other assessments: the comparison supports a decision, it never replaces the assessment. Anchor roles are whole roles; the evaluation scale on each criterion describes the ratings.
help.trackBody: A track is the kind of job, for example individual contributor or manager. It is set on the role and groups roles with the same kind of responsibility. It says nothing about seniority or how high the role lands.
help.familyBody: A role family groups roles that share the same kind of work, like Engineering or Sales. Families organize the role register and the results filter; they never affect the weighting.
help.weightingBody: Weighting is how much influence each criterion has on role valuation and, all else equal, on relative pay. It is not a verdict on whether a criterion is good or modern. Each criterion gets 1 to 5 weight points from one fixed budget for the whole model, so giving one more means the others count relatively less. New criteria start at the neutral 3.
help.scoreLabel: How are the weighting and band computed?
help.scoreBody: Every criterion is rated 0 to 5 and weighted by its weight points; the total is normalized to a fixed 0 to 100 scale, the weighting. The band then follows automatically from the weighting via the model's band thresholds, where band 1 is the highest. Neither can be set by hand.
help.blindRatingBody: Pick the level that best matches what the role requires, not how the current employee performs. Ratings, weighting, and band stay hidden until the last criterion so each one is judged on its own; the result appears as soon as every criterion is rated.
help.familiesReviewBody: A role family groups roles that share the same kind of work, like Engineering or Sales; families organize the register and never affect the weighting. A track is the kind of job, for example individual contributor or manager; it says nothing about seniority.
help.pendingBandLabel: Why does a role have no band yet?
help.pendingBandBody: A role gets a band only after every criterion is rated. Until the assessment is complete it has no weighting and no band, so it waits in this list.
help.contributionLabel: What do the bars and percentages show?
help.contributionBody: The percentage is each criterion's share of this role's total weighting (your rating times the criterion's weight in the model), so the criteria add up to 100%. The bars are scaled to the biggest contributor, so the top one fills the row.
help.twoFactorBody: Two-step verification adds a second check when you sign in, on top of your password: a code from an authenticator app or sent to your email. A stolen password alone then can't open your account. It is required for everyone on blueprnt.
help.changeEmailLabel: How does changing your email work?
help.changeEmailBody: Changing your email is a two-step process to keep your account secure. First, a confirmation link goes to your current address so only you can authorise the change. Then a verification link goes to your new address to confirm you own it. The change only takes effect once both links are clicked.
help.orgCurrencyBody: The currency your organization uses for pay. It defaults from your country and is used wherever amounts are shown. Changing it does not convert any existing values.
help.orgLanguageBody: The organization's default language, used for things like invitation emails when a member has not chosen their own. Each person can still pick their own display language.
help.methodAppendixBody: An exportable document with your criteria, weights, rationale, and bias review as compliance evidence.
help.biasReviewBody: A per-criterion check of gender and bias risk with a mitigating action. It shows the model is designed to reduce bias.
help.criterionRationaleBody: Why this criterion belongs in the model: its purpose, why it's relevant, and any overlap with others. It documents the objective basis the pay-transparency directive requires.
help.methodPurposeBody: What this criterion measures and the outcome it captures.
help.methodWhyRelevantBody: Why this criterion is relevant to the work's value, and why it is gender-neutral.
help.methodOverlapBody: Any overlap with other criteria, so the same thing is not weighted twice.
help.methodBiasRiskBody: The risk that this criterion over- or under-values gender-coded work, for example favouring visible mandate or budget size over actual complexity and responsibility.
help.methodBiasCommentBody: What you considered in the bias check and what you found.
help.methodBiasActionBody: The mitigating action taken to reduce the risk, such as reworded level descriptions or adjusted weighting.
